description: Externally-authored adversarial recall queries for the semantic_recall / abstention path. Two independent non-Claude models (grok-build, Fugu) wrote these blind to each other. SHOULD-ABSTAIN = topically alien to an agent-orchestration / memory-tool corpus (lexically may overlap on polysemous terms like 'agent', 'pipeline', 'branch', 'node'); a good recall engine returns nothing. SHOULD-RETURN = describes the corpus domain in synonyms / paraphrase / casual phrasing with little-to-no exact lexical overlap; a good engine still surfaces it. Labels assume a corpus of engram/agent-orchestration development notes; point the runner at your own store with --db. Keyword-only recall tops out ~64-66% here (the SHOULD-RETURN synonym cases + SHOULD-ABSTAIN polysemy cases are the semantic residual); the semantic_recall flag lifts it.
